element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Building huts, towers, and military structures; foundation for all unit training
Direct control unit; fragile but spell-casting capable; reincarnation mechanic
Tough melee combat unit for direct confrontation
Weak long-range offensive unit; risk-reward positioning
Conversion and defensive suppression of enemy preachers
Terrain deformation; land creation and destruction
Raising sea floor for strategic crossing; map manipulation
Offensive spell; enemy confusion and disruption
Later levels have multiple shamans on same world; escalating threat
Convert spell targets; passive resource for population expansion
Shaman must enter vault directly; risky but grants permanent spells
Same world contains multiple enemy shamans requiring coordinated elimination

typical_relief_triggers: Successful tribe elimination on a world
Spell acquisition from vault or totem
typical_tension_triggers: Rival shaman appearance on shared world
Multiple enemy shamans on same map
Enemy preacher conversion of player units
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Campaign progression through 25 planets creates escalating long-cycle tension; single-mission difficulty varies by objective type; real-time combat creates moment-to-moment micro-tensions around unit losses and spell availability

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Wildmen clusters around trees and water; neutral population signals resource-gathering opportunities
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Vaults contain permanent spell upgrades; require direct shaman traversal to unlock hidden power progression

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of basic unit types (Brave, Warrior) and foundational spells (Tornado, Storm); single enemy tribe per world; tutorial-adjacent mission design
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of specialized units (Preacher, Spy, Firewarrior); multiple spells available; vault and totem mechanics; enemy shamans begin appearing; some worlds feature alliances between enemy tribes
difficulty_curve.late_game: Multi-shaman worlds requiring coordination; full spell roster (26 total); enemy AI tribes with established infrastructure; escalating offensive complexity
notable_spikes: First multi-enemy-shaman world (requires new tactical approach)
Worlds with pre-allied enemy tribes (eliminates divide-and-conquer strategies)
Final campaign missions (full spell roster and unit variety required)

notable_level_patterns.description: Standard objective: dominate one enemy tribe on a single world; introduces unit training and basic combat
Multiple enemy shamans inhabit same world; requires simultaneous defense and offense; escalates mid-to-late campaign
Two or more enemy tribes form alliance; cannot be defeated individually; forces player to address defensive coordination
Water barriers require Landbridge spell or equivalent terrain manipulation; gates progression to spell acquisition moments
Population growth via Convert spell on neutral wildmen; alternative to purely military domination
